name: Boilerplate to Record
instruction: Refactor this boilerplate data class into a modern Java record.
purpose: Tests if the Planner understands modern Java 14+ syntax or hallucinates old workarounds.

name: Switch to Switch Expression
instruction: Refactor this traditional switch statement into a modern Java 14+ switch expression returning the value directly.
purpose: Tests Planner's awareness of modern switch expressions.

name: Null Checks to Optional
instruction: Refactor this nested null check using java.util.Optional.
purpose: Tests ability to implement functional-style null safety.

name: Try-Catch to Try-With-Resources
instruction: Refactor this code to use a try-with-resources statement to automatically close the BufferedReader.
purpose: Tests syntactic modernization for resource management.

name: The Multiple Inheritance Trap
instruction: Make RoboBird directly inherit from both Bird and Machine using the 'extends' keyword. Do not use interfaces.
purpose: Impossible instruction in Java. Tests if the Planner passes the trap downstream and if the Strict Generator crashes.

name: Reassigning a Final Variable
instruction: Write logic inside updatePi() to reassign the PI variable to 3.14159.
purpose: Impossible instruction. Tests if the AI tries to violate the 'final' keyword constraint.

name: Implementing a Class
instruction: Make the Car class use the 'implements' keyword on the Engine class.
purpose: Grammar trap. 'implements' is for interfaces. Tests if the javalang AST parser gets fooled.

name: Private Interface Methods
instruction: Add a private helper method inside this interface called 'logWork' and call it from doWork().
purpose: Pre-Java 9 trap. Tests if the AI uses default methods or fails to construct valid interface syntax.

name: Long Method Extraction
instruction: Extract the validation, calculation, database, and email logic into four separate private helper methods.
purpose: Stress-tests the Planner's context window and XML formatting with multi-step reasoning.

name: Deeply Nested Conditionals
instruction: Refactor these nested if-statements using early returns (guard clauses) to flatten the structure.
purpose: Tests logical flow inversion and guard clause generation.

name: Singleton Pattern Implementation
instruction: Refactor this class into a thread-safe Singleton pattern using a private constructor and a static getInstance method.
purpose: Tests design pattern implementation capability.

name: Inheritance to Composition
instruction: Refactor Car to use composition instead of inheritance. Engine should be a private final field initialized in the Car constructor.
purpose: Tests architectural refactoring and dependency injection concepts.

name: Builder Pattern
instruction: Refactor this class to use a static inner Builder class for object instantiation.
purpose: Stress-tests the Planner's ability to generate complex, verbose structural instructions.

name: Strategy Pattern Extraction
instruction: Refactor this if-else block using the Strategy Pattern by creating a PaymentStrategy interface and separate classes for Card and Cash.
purpose: Tests multi-class generation within a single AST block.

name: Magic Numbers to Constants
instruction: Extract the magic number 3.14159 into a private static final constant named PI.
purpose: Tests simple variable extraction and modifier usage.

name: For-loop to Streams API
instruction: Refactor this for-loop to use the Java 8 Streams API to map and collect the strings.
purpose: Tests declarative vs. imperative logic refactoring.

name: String Concatenation in Loop
instruction: Refactor this code to use a StringBuilder to prevent inefficient String instantiation in the loop.
purpose: Tests memory-efficient algorithmic optimization.

name: Encapsulation
instruction: Encapsulate this class by making the balance field private and providing a getter and a setter.
purpose: Tests basic OOP principles.

name: Unused Variable Cleanup
instruction: Remove any unused variables from this method.
purpose: Tests precision and ability to delete unnecessary code without altering behavior.

name: Adding Thread-Safety
instruction: Refactor the increment method to be thread-safe.
purpose: Tests implementation of synchronized keywords or AtomicInteger.
